basic_info.incident_name: Goodyear Heat Exchanger Rupture and Ammonia Release
basic_info.incident_name_cn: 固特异换热器破裂及氨泄漏事故
basic_info.company: The Goodyear Tire and Rubber Company

process_context.operating_conditions: Pressurized anhydrous ammonia in shell side (cooling medium); process fluids in tube side; operating near design pressure; steam cleaning conducted during maintenance

failure_mechanism.root_cause: Failure to reopen the isolation valve after rupture disk replacement, combined with subsequent steam heating of trapped liquid ammonia causing uncontrolled pressure buildup.
failure_mechanism.causal_chain: Isolation valve left closed after maintenance → ammonia trapped in shell → steam introduced into tubes → heat transfer to shell-side liquid ammonia → phase change and vapor pressure rise → no pressure relief path → overpressure beyond vessel MAWP → catastrophic rupture
failure_mechanism.physical_principles: Thermal expansion of liquid ammonia, vapor pressure-temperature relationship (Clausius-Clapeyron), adiabatic heating, mechanical failure under tensile stress exceeding ultimate tensile strength

existing_safeguards: Rupture disk and relief valve assembly
protection_system.safeguard_failures: Isolation valve remained closed post-maintenance, blocking both primary (relief valve) and secondary (pressure control valve) overpressure protection paths; no interlock or verification step ensured valve reopening; accountability system failed to detect missing worker due to poor lighting, debris, and procedural gaps.
csb_recommendations: Implement positive verification (e.g., checklist, electronic tagout, dual-person signoff) for critical valve repositioning after maintenance
Install pressure monitoring with high-pressure alarms and automatic shutdown on heat exchanger shells containing volatile refrigerants
Redesign overpressure protection to be inherently fail-safe (e.g., direct-mounted relief device without isolatable upstream valve)

embedding_text: Shell-and-tube heat exchanger ruptured due to overpressure caused by steam heating trapped liquid anhydrous ammonia; isolation valve remained closed after rupture disk replacement, disabling all pressure relief paths; pressure exceeded MAWP leading to violent failure, fatal projectile impact, and toxic ammonia release; root cause was procedural failure in maintenance completion and inadequate overpressure protection design.
